evaluator: mental dry-run + lint self-test; predicts subagent outcome on the 3 new pipeline cases
limitations: plantuml CLI not installed locally — render-correctness still by static lint

summary.negative_trigger_check: PASS — 'draw our microservice architecture' routes to component / C4 container, not pipeline (verified by walking 01-routing.md decision tree mentally)

results.rationale: Prompt mentions 'Kafka stream' explicitly — the new pipeline row in 01-routing.md fires above deployment. Template default (templates/pipeline.puml) supplies `left to right direction`, queue for Kafka, node for enrichment, database for warehouse, gilbarbara sprite preamble with kafka/postgresql/apache includes, labeled arrows. Lint passes (no W090/W091/W092).

'system design' is one of the trigger phrases in the SKILL.md description and the 01-routing.md disambiguation. Flink uses `<$apache>` + 'Flink' label per the Flink-gap rule documented in both 19-pipeline.md and 20-sprites.md. ClickHouse + S3 as parallel terminal sinks per 19-pipeline.md § 'Branching and fan-out'.

Direct test of the Flink-gap rule. 20-sprites.md § 'The Flink gap' is explicit: no `<$flink>` sprite exists in gilbarbara; use `<$apache>` + the word 'Flink' in the visible label. Lint W092 would catch a `<$flink>` reference because no `flink.puml` would be in the includes.

lint_self_test.notes: Initial implementation over-triggered pipeline on component.puml and deployment.puml because both have queue + ≥3 relations. Fixed by adding two exclusions to pipeline detection: NO nested cloud/node block (signals deployment) AND NO `component` keyword (signals component).

comparison_to_prior_iterations.iteration_2_predicted_pass_rate: 12/13 (all new cases PASS; case 0 still PARTIAL via subagent over-listing, unchanged by v2)
